credit-card.notes: Illinois applies a 5-year limitation period to written contracts including credit card agreements. The Illinois Collection Agency Act regulates collectors and mirrors many FDCPA protections.
credit-card.citation: 735 ILCS 5/13-205; 225 ILCS 425/1 et seq. (Collection Agency Act)

medical.notes: Medical debts based on written contracts are subject to Illinois's 5-year limitation. Medical malpractice has a 2-year SOL, but billing disputes follow contract law.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances follow Illinois's 5-year written contract limitation from the date of the deficiency.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Illinois are subject to the 5-year limitation for written contracts.

student-loan.notes: Private student loans in Illinois are subject to the 5-year limitation. Federal loans are exempt from state SOL for government collection.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Illinois has a 7-year limitation on certain mortgage-related claims under the real property provisions. Consult an Illinois attorney as deficiency rules vary by foreclosure type.

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts in Illinois are subject to the standard 5-year limitation for written contracts.

regulatoryBody: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR) & Illinois Attorney General
complaints: File a complaint with IDFPR at idfpr.illinois.gov or the IL AG at illinoisattorneygeneral.gov. Illinois's Collection Agency Act (225 ILCS 425) requires collectors to be licensed.
